mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Ni(0) species generated through β-hydride elimination

chemistry.scope_summary: Electron-poor, electron-neutral, and electron-rich aryl bromides; heteroaryl bromides (pyridine, pyrimidine, pyrazole, quinoline, indole, indazole); electron-deficient aryl chlorides; primary amines, secondary cyclic amines, anilines (with Et3N as hydride source)

optimization_table.key_findings: Best conditions: Ru(0.2) 3.0 equiv DABCO (2.0) 450 nm 80°C 0.5 M giving 78% yield

figures.key_information: dual catalysis system using nickel catalyst and photoredox catalyst under light irradiation for C-N coupling of aryl halides with amines, achieving up to 90% yield across 25 examples
figures.caption: Figure 1. Photoredox and nickel-catalyzed aniline formation: comparison of batch and flow processes.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction times (240 min) with moderate yields (42%) under lower temperature conditions
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Reduced residence time (60 min), improved yields (78%) at higher temperature (80°C) with continuous processing and better heat transfer
translation_logic.what_changed: Temperature increased from 55°C to 80°C, light wavelength from broad blue to 450 nm, reaction time reduced from 240 min to 60 min residence time
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: DMSO solvent, 0.5 M concentration, DABCO base, dual catalysis system (Ru photocatalyst + Ni catalyst)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow processing enables better temperature control and reduced exposure to reaction mixture

figure_descriptions: Comparison of batch vs flow photoredox catalysis setup
Substrate scope table showing 18 examples with yields 40-98%

conflicts: Photocatalyst loading: Pass 2 shows 0.002 mol% but Pass 3 table shows 0.02 mol% - using table value
notes: High confidence extraction with detailed optimization table data. Minor conflict resolved by prioritizing tabulated values over text mentions. Scale-up to 10 mmol demonstrated successfully.
